macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Indexes Decline Amid Pullback from Record Highs
items.summary: The Nasdaq fell 0.2% and the Dow Jones dropped 557 points, pulling back from recent record highs, driven by market pressures including inflation concerns and Middle East tensions.

items.title: Gas Prices Surge to $4.536 Nationally Due to Iran Tensions and Strait of Hormuz Closure
items.summary: AAA reports national average gas price at $4.536, with Los Angeles nearing $9 per gallon, amid US-Iran conflict disrupting oil supplies; Chevron CEO warns of global shortages.

items.title: US ADP Employment Change Beats Expectations at 109K Jobs
items.summary: Private payrolls rose by 109K in the latest report, surpassing economist estimates of 99K, though prior month revised lower to 62K.

items.title: Q1 GDP Grows 2%, Slightly Below Expectations
items.summary: US first quarter GDP expanded at 2%, missing forecasts of 2.3%, supported by AI equipment investment and consumer spending but tempered by other factors.

items.title: PCE Inflation Rises and Remains Elevated Due to Iran Conflict
items.summary: Latest Commerce Department data shows March PCE inflation increased, partly due to impacts from the Iran war and rising fuel costs.

items.title: Trump Warns of Strikes on Iran Oil Infrastructure if No Deal Reached
items.summary: President Trump threatens targeting Iran's electric plants, oil wells, and Kharg Island unless a deal is made and the Strait of Hormuz is reopened.

items.title: RBA Minutes Signal Further Tightening as Inflation Risks Rise to 5%
items.summary: Reserve Bank of Australia minutes indicate expectations of more rate hikes, with inflation potentially hitting 5% in Q2 if oil prices stay near $100.

items.title: 30-Year Mortgage Rate Rises to 6.3%
items.summary: Freddie Mac data shows the average 30-year fixed mortgage rate increased to 6.3% from 6.23% the prior week.

items.title: US Public Debt Surpasses 100% of GDP for First Time Since WWII
items.summary: National debt held by the public reached over 100% of GDP in March, exceeding the size of the economy amid ongoing fiscal pressures.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Ripple Prices Fall Despite US-Iran Peace Optimism
items.summary: Bitcoin fell 1.2% to approximately $80,800, while Ethereum dropped over 3% and XRP fell 2.7% over the past 24 hours, despite global stock markets reaching record highs following optimism about a potential US-Iran peace agreement. The cryptocurrency market has lost momentum with Bitcoin down 7% year-to-date and trading 55% below its October 2025 all-time high.

items.title: $253 Million in Crypto Long Positions Liquidated in 24 Hours
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market experienced significant volatility with over $253 million in long positions liquidated over 24 hours, affecting major cryptocurrencies including Bitcoin and large-cap altcoins. The liquidation event highlighted continued risks associated with leveraged trading across digital asset markets.

items.title: Zcash Surges to 18th Largest Cryptocurrency as Privacy Coins Rally
items.summary: Zcash (ZEC) has surged to $413 with a market capitalization exceeding $6.9 billion, ranking as the 18th-largest cryptocurrency globally, driven by protocol upgrades and institutional reallocation rather than retail trading. The rally represents a significant recovery for privacy coins following regulatory delisting pressures in 2023-2024.

items.title: Bitcoin Pulls Back After Three-Month High as Market Volatility Continues
items.summary: Bitcoin declined 1.5% to around $80,800 after reaching above $82,000 earlier in the week, with the broader crypto market down 1.4% and over $420 million in leveraged positions liquidated. Dogecoin and Ethereum also experienced significant declines of 4.4% and 3.6% respectively.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: SubQ: New AI Model Claims to Outperform Claude, GPT, and Gemini
items.summary: A new large language model called SubQ has been introduced featuring a sub-quadratic sparse-attention architecture, claiming to outperform frontier models like Claude Opus 4.6/4.7 and GPT 5.4/5.5 while being 52x faster than flash attention implementations.

items.title: Unity Launches AI Open Beta for Game Developers
items.summary: Unity has released an AI Open Beta designed to support developers in production workflows, helping teams iterate faster and solve development challenges while maintaining developer control over the technology.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: US Tech Sector Rebounds Strongly on US-Iran Peace Expectations
items.summary: The US tech sector and broader market surged in the past 24 hours, driven by optimism over US-Iran peace negotiations and potential Strait of Hormuz reopening, with Nasdaq and S&P 500 hitting new highs. Strong earnings from AMD and Super Micro Computer fueled the rally, lifting the Dow out of correction territory.

items.title: TCS Wins 5-Year NHS Supply Chain IT Modernization Deal
items.summary: Tata Consultancy Services secured a 5-year contract with NHS Supply Chain to modernize IT systems using cloud solutions, enhancing operational efficiency.

items.title: Lenovo India Surpasses $2 Billion Revenue, Expands Manufacturing
items.summary: Lenovo India announced exceeding $2 billion in revenue and revealed multiple projects focused on manufacturing expansion at Tech World India 2025.

items.title: Indian IT Firms Boost Productivity with Higher Revenue per Employee
items.summary: HCLTech led top IT firms with a 2% rise in revenue per employee to over $16,000 in Q2, followed by gains at TCS and Tech Mahindra amid headcount adjustments.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX Plans $119 Billion Terafab Chip Plant
items.summary: SpaceX is developing a massive Terafab chip manufacturing plant estimated at $119 billion, advancing vertical integration in hardware supply chains.

items.title: SpaceX Prepares for Public Listing at Over $1 Trillion Valuation
items.summary: Elon Musk is gearing up to take SpaceX public with an estimated valuation exceeding $1 trillion, following his departure from Trump's White House in 2025.
